package repository

import (
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository returns a framework builder (*gorm.DB or *sql.DB)
// to the upper layer instead of returning concrete results or domain types.

type Task struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Title    string `gorm:"column:title"`
	Status   string `gorm:"column:status"`
	Priority int    `gorm:"column:priority"`
}

type TaskRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// GetQueryBuilder returns *gorm.DB to the service layer - anti-pattern.
// The caller now has direct access to the query builder.
func (r *TaskRepository) GetQueryBuilder() *gorm.DB {
	return r.db
}

// FilterByStatus returns a *gorm.DB chain instead of []Task - anti-pattern.
func (r *TaskRepository) FilterByStatus(status string) *gorm.DB {
	return r.db.Where("status = ?", status)
}

// GetDB returns the raw *gorm.DB instance - anti-pattern.
func (r *TaskRepository) GetDB() *gorm.DB {
	return r.db
}

// Example misuse in service layer:
//
//	qb := repo.GetQueryBuilder()
//	qb.Where("priority > ?", 5).Delete(&Task{})
//
// The service layer can now execute arbitrary queries, bypassing
// repository encapsulation.
